_comment: Single source of truth for the org model/effort policy (docs/design/2026-07-17-automation-structure-and-anti-drift.md, propose row + Model & effort knobs). Setpoint updated 2026-07-21 from 'sonnet pinned org-wide, no Opus escalation' to SONNET-FIRST + effort-gating: sonnet+medium default for the auto-lane, effort:large GATED to human (see effort_gating below), one Opus+high retry on turn-cap failure (see turncap_retry — RATIFIED, impl pending F2b). Gated by scripts/test-model-policy.sh (#369) so the reusable defaults, self-*/scaffolded caller-stub pins, budget-governor's opus classification, and the effort-gate floor can't silently re-drift from each other the way they already did once: fixer.yml's `model` default flipped opus->sonnet in #373, but budget-governor.yml's OPUS_WF_RE and its test still expected the bare 'Self fixer'/'Fixer' name to classify as opus for two days until #369 caught it.

escalation_opt_in_only._comment: issue-build.yml's sensedOpus escalation only runs the MAIN model as opus when a caller explicitly sets model-hint to escalation_value below; no caller may pin that value by default.

effort_gating._comment: 2026-07-21 setpoint: effort:large is GATED to human approval — added to the shared nonbuildable-labels floor so large work of ANY type (incl. bugs) is NOT auto-built; it stays open for a human go-ahead. Rationale: a large issue just exhausts the Sonnet turn cap and lands needs-human anyway, so gating is cheaper than escalating. Asserted against the action's emitted floor so the policy and the gate can't drift.

turncap_retry._comment: 2026-07-21 setpoint: opus-on-fail. RATIFIED; implementation PENDING (F2b). One bounded Opus+high retry when an auto-lane build fails specifically on turn-cap exhaustion (stream-json result subtype 'error_max_turns', matched STRUCTURALLY — never a text grep, cf. classify-security-noverdict.sh — so an issue/tool-output string can't spoof a costly retry). NOT a blanket escalation and NOT a retry on real test/security rejections. status=pending until the retry step lands in issue-build.yml, so scripts/test-model-policy.sh does not yet assert it against code.
